Name: Fuel Tax
Effects: Raises weekly revenue and slightly reduces car use.
Synergies: Climate Communication Campaign; Electric Bus Purchase; Fuel Price Spike
Risks: Backlash during driver tax revolt; revenue falls with EV adoption.

Name: Congestion Charge
Effects: Reduces peak car trips into priced zones and generates revenue.
Synergies: Congestion Charge Gantries; Bus Priority Lane; License Plate Camera Network
Risks: High backlash without visible transit alternatives.
Acquisition notes: Draft policy or unlocked by toll abilities.

Name: Congestion Dividend
Effects: Improves approval when congestion charge revenue funds fares or local projects.
Synergies: Congestion Charge; Low-Income Fare Pass; Pop-Up Pedestrian Plaza
Risks: Less net revenue retained for capital.

Name: Extra Transit Subsidy
Effects: Increases weekly budget for transit operating costs.
Synergies: Low-Income Fare Pass; Night Bus Service; Subway Frequency Increase
Risks: Can mask inefficient service if overused.

Name: Night Shift Economy
Effects: Spreads commute demand across more hours, lowering peak congestion.
Synergies: Night Bus Service; Industrial Shift Shuttle; Hospital Access Shuttle
Risks: Requires stronger off-peak service to capture benefits.

Name: Work From Home Act
Effects: Reduces office peak demand and increases local midday trips.
Synergies: Telework Outreach Campaign; Employer Transit Pass Pilot; Remote Work Trend
Risks: Can reduce fare revenue on commuter routes.

Name: Parking Cash-Out
Effects: Employers must offer cash instead of free parking, reducing drive-alone commuting.
Synergies: Employer Transit Pass Pilot; Parking Meter Expansion; Mobility Wallet Pilot
Acquisition notes: Oil price crash or tech campus event.

Name: Parking Minimum Repeal
Effects: New development requires less parking, reducing future car ownership pressure.
Synergies: Remove Minimum Parking Lot; Transit-Oriented Zoning Hearing; Mixed-Use Retrofit
Risks: Can worsen spillover parking without curb pricing.

Name: Dynamic Parking Pricing
Effects: Raises or lowers parking prices based on demand; reduces cruising.
Synergies: Curb Pricing Pilot; Smart Parking Guidance; Event Parking Levy
Acquisition notes: Draft or unlocked by curb pricing.

Name: Event Parking Levy
Effects: Charges extra parking fees near major venues during events.
Synergies: Special Event Transit Plan; Championship Game; Convention Week
Risks: Can shift parking spillover to neighborhoods.

Name: Low-Emission Zone
Effects: Restricts high-polluting vehicles in dense areas; lowers smog penalties.
Synergies: License Plate Camera Network; Electric Bus Purchase; Vehicle Electrification Grant
Risks: May burden low-income drivers without exemptions.

Name: Road Usage Charge
Effects: Replaces declining fuel tax with distance-based revenue.
Synergies: Electric Vehicle Adoption Wave; License Plate Camera Network; Congestion Charge

Name: Vehicle Electrification Grant
Effects: Subsidizes electric buses, taxis, and delivery vehicles.
Synergies: Electric Bus Purchase; Cargo Bike Delivery Hub; Low-Emission Zone
Risks: Does not reduce traffic volume by itself.
Acquisition notes: EV events or climate policy draft.
Notes: Good with smog but needs mode shift too.

Name: Climate Mobility Mandate
Effects: Increases draft rate of transit, bike, and walking abilities; reduces road expansion support.
Synergies: Protected Bike Lane; Subway Extension 1KM; Green Bond Issuance

Name: Green Bond Authority
Effects: Allows borrowing for low-emission capital projects.
Synergies: Green Bond Issuance; Subway Extension 1KM; Commuter Rail Electrification Segment

Name: Rainy Day Transit Fund
Effects: Stores a portion of surplus budget and protects against recession shocks.
Synergies: Budget Windfall; Recession; Preventive Maintenance Lockbox

Name: Countercyclical Infrastructure
Effects: During recession, capital projects cost less and approval from job creation rises.
Synergies: Recession; Green Bond Authority; Construction Coordination Office
Risks: Can encourage overbuilding with weak operating budget.

Name: Preventive Maintenance Lockbox
Effects: Earmarks revenue for road and transit maintenance, reducing breakdown and pothole events.
Synergies: Pothole Repair Blitz; Maintenance Overtime; Spare Parts Stockpile

Name: Fleet Renewal Mandate
Effects: Aging buses and trains are replaced before reliability penalties grow.
Synergies: Electric Bus Purchase; Spare Parts Stockpile; Maintenance Overtime

Name: Operator Training Academy
Effects: Improves hiring pipeline and reduces operator shortage duration.
Synergies: Bus Driver Hiring Fair; Night Bus Service; Tram Vehicle

Name: Labor Peace Accord
Effects: Reduces strike risk but increases transit operating costs.
Synergies: Transit Strike Vote; Extra Transit Subsidy; Bus Driver Hiring Fair

Name: Transparent Procurement
Effects: Reduces corruption and megaproject cost overruns.
Synergies: Open Mobility Data Portal; Federal Grant Application; Subway Extension 1KM

Name: Public Performance Dashboard
Effects: Improves approval when reliability is good; reveals poor performance more sharply.
Synergies: Open Mobility Data Portal; Data Science Hiring Sprint; Transit Operations Center

Name: Evidence-Based Planning
Effects: Advisor recommendations better match observed demand.
Synergies: Neighborhood Mobility Survey; Data Science Hiring Sprint; Demand-Responsive Shuttle

Name: Advisor Diversity Mandate
Effects: Increases variety of future ability offers across modes.
Synergies: Advisor Recruitment Fair; Neighborhood Mobility Survey; Federal Grant Application
Risks: Less chance to tunnel on one strategy.

Name: Shovel-Ready Project Bank
Effects: Improves chance of receiving and using grants quickly.
Synergies: Federal Grant Application; Transit-Oriented Zoning Hearing; Construction Coordination Office
Risks: Requires planning spending before grants arrive.

Name: Matching Grant Office
Effects: Increases federal and provincial grant success for transit capital.
Synergies: Federal Transit-Friendly Win; Subway Station; Commuter Rail Station

Name: Local Funding Autonomy
Effects: Reduces dependence on higher governments and unlocks local taxes or fees.
Synergies: Congestion Charge; Green Bond Authority; Road Usage Charge
Risks: Local voters blame you directly for costs.

Name: Political Capital Reserve
Effects: Stores unused approval to spend on controversial future policies.
Synergies: Public Consultation Roadshow; Congestion Charge; Road Diet
Risks: Can tempt delay when action is needed.

Name: Community Benefit Agreement
Effects: Reduces backlash from large projects in affected neighborhoods.
Synergies: Subway Station; Dedicated Tram Right-of-Way; Low-Traffic Neighborhood Trial

Name: Complete Streets Ordinance
Effects: Road projects must include safety, walking, biking, or transit improvements.
Synergies: Road Diet; Protected Bike Lane; Bus Bulb Construction
Risks: Pure car capacity becomes harder to build.

Name: Complete Streets Funding Rule
Effects: External road funds can be converted into multimodal corridors.
Synergies: Provincial Highway Promise; Bus Priority Lane; Protected Bike Lane
Notes: Turns car offers into flexible offers.

Name: Vision Zero
Effects: Reduces crash events and increases support for traffic calming and protected bike projects.
Synergies: Speed Limit Reduction; Protected Intersection; Road Diet
Risks: Car speeds and some throughput decline.

Name: Safe School Streets
Effects: Improves school travel plans and reduces school peak car trips city-wide.
Synergies: School Travel Plan; Crosswalk Upgrade; Traffic Calming Package
Risks: Parent backlash if access changes are abrupt.

Name: Universal Mobility Standard
Effects: Accessibility upgrades produce larger approval and ridership benefits.
Synergies: Accessible Curb Ramp Program; Paratransit Dispatch Upgrade; Senior Shuttle Program
Risks: Raises cost of stations and streetscape projects.

Name: Equity Fare Cap
Effects: Limits fare burden for low-income riders and improves transit adoption.
Synergies: Low-Income Fare Pass; Mobility Wallet Pilot; Extra Transit Subsidy

Name: Rider Bill of Rights
Effects: Delay compensation improves trust but penalizes poor reliability financially.
Synergies: Transit Operations Center; Maintenance Overtime; Public Performance Dashboard

Name: Proof-of-Payment Reform
Effects: Makes all-door boarding and off-board payment more effective with less trust loss.
Synergies: All-Door Boarding Pilot; Off-Board Fare Payment; Targeted Fare Inspection

Name: Fare Integration
Effects: Transfers across modes are cheaper and more attractive.
Synergies: Transit Fare Integration Study; Integrated Wayfinding Signs; Regional Rail Through-Running
Risks: May reduce fare revenue without subsidy.

Name: Regional Fare Compact
Effects: Commuter rail, bus, and city transit share fares and passes.
Synergies: Commuter Rail Station; Park-and-Ride Lot; Fare Integration

Name: Visitor Transit Pass
Effects: Tourists receive simple transit passes; reduces taxi and rental-car trips.
Synergies: Airport Express Bus; Tourism Shuttle; Convention Week

Name: Campus Mobility Compact
Effects: Universities fund U-Pass, bike share, and move-in traffic plans.
Synergies: Student U-Pass Expansion; Bike Share Dock; Night Bus Service
Risks: Budget help depends on campus presence.

Name: Employer TDM Mandate
Effects: Large employers must reduce drive-alone commuting.
Synergies: Employer Transit Pass Pilot; Vanpool Program; Work From Home Act
Risks: Business opposition if transit is weak.

Name: Hospital Shift Mobility
Effects: Hospitals coordinate passes, shuttles, and staggered shifts.
Synergies: Hospital Access Shuttle; Night Shift Economy; Senior Shuttle Program
Risks: Narrow unless hospitals are major trip generators.

Name: Freight Priority Compact
Effects: Coordinates freight routes and delivery timing to reduce peak conflicts.
Synergies: Freight Signal Priority; Downtown Delivery Windows; Urban Freight Consolidation Center
Risks: Can prioritize freight over neighborhood comfort.

Name: Port Mobility Compact
Effects: Port operators co-fund worker shuttles and freight improvements.
Synergies: Industrial Shift Shuttle; Commuter Rail Station; Freight Signal Priority

Name: Downtown Delivery Windows
Effects: Moves commercial deliveries out of peak commute hours.
Synergies: Cargo Bike Delivery Hub; Curb Pricing Pilot; Freight Priority Compact

Name: Ridehail Curb Fee
Effects: Charges ridehail pickups in crowded zones and funds transit operations.
Synergies: Ridehail Geofence; Curb Pricing Pilot; Event Parking Levy
Risks: Can increase illegal stopping without enforcement.

Name: Open Streets Program
Effects: Regular car-free street days increase walking, biking, and public support.
Synergies: Pop-Up Pedestrian Plaza; Car-Free Day Proposal; Main Street Sidewalk Widening
Risks: Backlash if transit access is poor.

Name: Low-Traffic Neighborhood Framework
Effects: Makes LTN trials cheaper and less backlash-prone.
Synergies: Low-Traffic Neighborhood Trial; Traffic Calming Package; Community Benefit Agreement
Risks: Can shift traffic to boundary roads.

Name: Transit-Oriented Development
Effects: New density near transit produces more riders and less car growth.
Synergies: Transit-Oriented Zoning Hearing; Subway Station; Tram Stop
Risks: Can increase crowding if service lags.

Name: Inclusionary TOD
Effects: Transit-oriented growth includes affordable housing, increasing equitable ridership.
Synergies: Transit-Oriented Development; Low-Income Fare Pass; Commuter Rail Station

Name: Mixed-Use Retrofit
Effects: Car-oriented retail areas can redevelop into walkable centers.
Synergies: Mall Redevelopment; Parking Minimum Repeal; Bus Line Extension

Name: Brownfield TOD
Effects: Closed industrial sites near transit can become housing or mixed-use districts.
Synergies: Factory Closure; Transit-Oriented Zoning Hearing; Commuter Rail Station

Name: Shade Streets
Effects: Walking and biking penalties from heat are reduced.
Synergies: Main Street Sidewalk Widening; Neighborhood Greenway; Heat Wave
Risks: Capital improvements compete with mobility projects.

Name: Winter Cycling Maintenance
Effects: Bike lanes and sidewalks retain more usefulness during snow.
Synergies: Protected Bike Lane; Snow Route Prep; Neighborhood Greenway
Notes: Makes cycling viable in harsh maps.

Name: Public Data Rights
Effects: Tech pilots must share useful data with the city.
Synergies: Autonomous Shuttle Vendor Pitch; Open Mobility Data Portal; Data Science Hiring Sprint
Notes: Keeps pilots from being pure flavor.

Name: Olympic Mobility Authority
Effects: Temporary authority speeds event transit projects and street management.
Synergies: Special Event Transit Plan; Subway Frequency Increase; Tourism Shuttle

Name: Emergency Street Powers
Effects: During disasters, temporary bus lanes, car bans, and detours can be enacted quickly.
Synergies: Heavy Snowstorm; Flash Flood; VIP Motorcade Detour Plan

Name: Service Guarantee
Effects: Raises minimum service on core routes; improves ridership trust.
Synergies: Extra Transit Subsidy; Transit Operations Center; Bus Depot Expansion

Name: Frequent Grid Standard
Effects: Bus lines with enough demand receive reliable all-day frequency bonuses.
Synergies: Bus Line Split; Add Standard Bus; Bus Priority Lane
Risks: Weak in low-density maps without feeders.
Notes: Turns bus network into a system.

Name: Mobility Wallet
Effects: Residents can spend credits on transit, bike share, car share, or shuttles.
Synergies: Mobility Wallet Pilot; Low-Income Fare Pass; Car Share Pod
